channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Trump’s China tariffs spark viral TikTok work-arounds
item.description: President Trump’s trade wars have officially landed on TikTok. U.S. TikTok users’ For You Pages are being flooded with videos from Chinese manufacturers urging Americans to bypass sweeping new tariffs by purchasing goods directly from China.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg wraps up testimony in antitrust case
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg emphasized in federal court on Wednesday that he bought Instagram and WhatsApp because he saw value in the companies — not to take out competitors, as the Federal Trade Commission alleges in a historic antitrust trial.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Tatooine-like planet outside the solar system may orbit two failed stars, scientists say
item.description: Scientists may have found a new Tatooine-like planet outside the solar system that orbits two failed stars.
item.dc:creator: Adithi Ramakrishnan, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: OpenAI picks labor icon Dolores Huerta and other philanthropy advisers as it moves toward for-profit
item.description: OpenAI has named labor leader Dolores Huerta and three others to a temporary advisory board that will help guide the artificial intelligence company’s philanthropy as it attempts to shift itself into a for-profit business.
item.dc:creator: Matt O'Brien, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Car rental startup Turo cuts 15% of workers after icing IPO
item.description: Car-rental startup Turo Inc. is cutting about 150 jobs after withdrawing plans for an initial public offering.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Deezer is rolling out its answer to Wrapped and Replay, plus three new features you won't find on Spotify or Apple Music
item.description: Deezer's launching four new features between now and the end of May – and top of the pile has to be platform-agnostic music sharing
item.dc:creator: becky.scarrott@futurenet.com (Becky Scarrott), TechRadar

item.title: Nvidia Announces Plans to Make AI Supercomputers in US for First Time - Latest AI Advancements
item.description: Nvidia announces plans to make AI supercomputers in US, smarter dairy farms with robots milking cows, and a 4-legged hydrogen-powered robot that can be ridden.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tech shares fall after Nvidia says new US controls on exports of AI chip will cost it $5.5 billion
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Shares in computer chipmakers slumped Wednesday after Nvidia said tighter U.S. government controls on exports of computer chips used for artificial intelligence will cost it an extra $5.5 billion.
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korean full-stack AI startup Upstage eyes expansion in the US and Japan
item.description: South Korean AI startup Upstage is ramping up global expansion in the US and Japan, positioning its full-stack AI platform to serve enterprise clients.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Windows 11 is about to get a nifty shortcut for copying out all the text from an image
item.description: Got an image on your monitor that you want to extract the text from? Windows 11 is about to make that process dead easy.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Google search ditches country-specific domains, directs all users to google.com
item.description: With search results now displaying localized content, Google will be redirecting all visits through google.com.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Major U.S. banks pause data sharing with this federal bureau after a cyberattack exposed sensitive information
item.description: Several of the largest U.S. banks are reportedly pausing or reassessing how they send sensitive information to the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency (OCC) following a major cyberattack on the regulator.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Cohere’s Embed 4 model helps enterprises search dynamic documents, ‘messy’ data
item.description: Embedding models help transform complex data — text, images, audio, and video — into numerical representations that computers can understand. The embeddings capture the semantic meaning of the data, making them useful for tasks like search, recommendation systems, and natural language processing.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: 8 times Meta has been accused of copying competitors’ features
item.description: Mark Zuckerberg’s marathon stint on the stand in the Federal Trade Commission’s (FTC) antitrust trial against Meta—the parent company of WhatsApp, Instagram, and Facebook—has been eye-opening for several reasons.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: Meta CEO Zuckerberg considered spinning off Instagram in 2018 over antitrust worries, email says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg once considered separating Instagram from its parent company due to worries about antitrust litigation, according to an email shown Tuesday on the second day of an antitrust trial alleging Meta illegally monopolized the social media market.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Zuckerberg defends Meta’s Instagram, WhatsApp deals in U.S. antitrust trial
item.description: Meta Platforms CEO Mark Zuckerberg took the stand on Monday at a high-stakes trial in Washington over U.S. antitrust enforcers’ claims that the company spent billions of dollars to acquire Instagram and WhatsApp to fend off Facebook competitors.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Bankrupt 23andMe faces scrutiny by U.S. House Committee as government seeks testimony from cofounder
item.description: A U.S. House committee on Tuesday asked 23andMe’s cofounder to testify next month as it launched an investigation into the risk of genetic data being transferred to potential buyers amid the DNA testing company’s bankruptcy.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Seth Rogen calls out Silicon Valley's Trump support in cut awards-show remarks: Report
item.description: Seth Rogen's remarks critical of President Trump and his support from tech executives at a ceremony "honoring scientists as heroes of our society" were reportedly cut from a stream of the event.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, The Hill

item.title: China pursuing 3 alleged US operatives over cyberattacks during Asian Games
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — China said Tuesday it is pursuing three alleged U.S. operatives accused of carrying out cyberattacks on Chinese infrastructure during the Asian Games held in the city of Harbin in February. A notice from the Harbin police headquarters named them as Katheryn A. Wilson, Robert J.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER BODEEN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Microsoft releases out-of-band updates to fix reporting error
item.description: Microsoft has released emergency patches to fix an apparent reporting error in Active Directory (AD) Group Policy, which allows administrators to manage and configure user and computer settings in Windows.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Accelerate Together
item.description: Use APIs to understand cloud vulnerabilities, recommends the public sector solutions engineering lead for Wiz. The post Accelerate Together — Zero Trust 2025: Wiz’s Chris Saunders on extending ZTA to cloud first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Nvidia plans to manufacture AI chips in the US for the first time
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Nvidia announced Monday that it will produce its artificial intelligence super computers in the United States for the first time.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Lawmakers Call for Investigation into Unauthorized Government Communications
item.description: Democrats on the Senate Homeland Security Committee demand answers from the National Archives regarding the use of Signal and Gmail by high-ranking government officials, citing security risks and potential law violations.
item.dc:creator: Michele Sandiford, Federal News Network

item.title: Meta says it will resume AI training with public content from European users
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Social media company Meta said Monday that it will start using publicly available content from European users to train its artificial intelligence models, resuming work put on hold last year after activists raised concerns about data privacy.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: As legacy of Ford fades in Brazil, China's BYD looks to extend global lead in EV sales
item.description: CAMACARI, Brazil (AP) — When Chinese automaker BYD Co. announced plans to build a massive factory in the hardscrabble city of Camacari, in Brazil’s northeastern Bahia state, locals saw a new beginning. After years of economic stagnation following Ford Motor Co.
item.dc:creator: FABIANO MAISONNAVE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Japanese leader says he hopes Osaka expo will help reunite a divided world
item.description: OSAKA, Japan (AP) — Japanese Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba, speaking at Saturday's opening ceremony for the Osaka expo, expressed his hope that the event will help restore global unity in a world plagued by conflicts and trade wars.
item.dc:creator: AYAKA McGILL and MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How thieves use new credit card numbers before you receive them
item.description: The CyberGuy provides tips to protect yourself from criminals who use various methods to make unauthorized transactions using your account information.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: One Tech Tip: Locking down your device when crossing borders
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Planning an international trip? Travelers should prepare for the possibility of extra scrutiny of their phones when crossing borders, especially when entering the United States. The Canadian government warned travelers in a recent travel advisory that U.S.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KRON4
